text:
Compromise (, rom. Konpuromaizu) is a spell card found in the Greed Island game. It allows a player to destroy one target player's A-Rank or above card, who can replace it with one 3 ranks lower of their choice.
__NOTOC__

| colspan="3" align="center" |Choose and destroy one above-A ranked card from target player. That player then obtains a card of 3 ranks lower than the destroyed card and of the number of his choosing. If target player has no above-A card, “Compromise” changes into card form again and returns to the caster. Summary

This spell allows for the caster to destroy a single A-Rank or above card from the target player. As a result, that player gets the right to pick any card of their choice to replace it with, provided that it is 3 ranks lower than the one destroyed. The spell fails if the target player has no A-Rank cards in their binder, in which case it will return to the caster in card form. Generally speaking, this card is useful for blackmail, and makes it harder for any unprotected player to obtain and sustain high rank cards. The silver lining is that the target player can choose a lower rank card that they need; however, if that card's conversion limit has already been reached, the target will be left with no card at all.

Card Description

{| class="article-table" style="height: 10px; color: #1A004C; background-color: #EEFFEE" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" border="0"
![Jap]
|No.1023 :: 
対象プレイヤー１名のランクＡ以上のカードから１枚選んで破壊する。破壊されたプレイヤーは、かわりに破壊されたカードの３ランク下の好きな番号のカードを得る。対象プレイヤーにＡ以上のカードがない場合、「妥協」は再びカード化しプレイヤーに戻る。（指定した３ランク下のカードの限界枚数がＭＡＸだったの場合カードはもらえない。）
|-
![Eng]
|No.1023 :: Compromise 
Pick 1 card not lower than A-Rank from 1 named target player to be destroyed. The destroyed card player is able to select any numbered card 3 ranks lower that they like. In the event the target player has no more A-Rank cards, "Compromise" is returned to the user once again. (In the event the card 3 ranks lower's transformation limit is MAX, you cannot receive it.)
|}

Trivia

"Compromise" is the only spell not to be destroyed if it fails.
This card was mentioned in Chapter 132 when Puhat is asked to list all the spell cards that it can destroy and that can be generated in return.
"Compromise" is also mentioned in Chapter 150 when Killua lists possible ways to obtain "Paladin's Necklace".
